system_files_touched.note: Out-of-repo system surface. Modified via Edit tool with root privileges. Cron at /etc/cron.d/tmp-cleanup-daily still triggers it daily at 02:07 (schedule unchanged).

tasks_completed.description: Created L1 SessionStart hook (session-tmpfs-banner.sh) — 6th entry in SessionStart hooks block
tasks_completed.rationale: AC1+AC2 — emit one line per mount via `df -h /tmp /dev/shm | tail -n +2`, with literal `WARN ` (5-byte) prefix on any mount whose Use% > 75. PATH-resolved bare `df` per OBJ-5. Outer `timeout 2s` so hung df cannot block SessionStart sequence. Smoke-tested via PATH-shim: 2 lines no-WARN at 46%/64%; WARN prefix per-line on over-threshold mounts; both-mounts-pressured case emits two WARN lines.

tasks_completed.description: Created L1.5 UserPromptSubmit hook (userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh) — new 4th block in UserPromptSubmit hooks array
tasks_completed.rationale: AC3 — non-blocking warning when /tmp and/or /dev/shm exceed 75%, with rate-limit ≤3 emissions per session. Token derivation: stdin JSON session_id → CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env → sha256(PPID+proc_start)[:16]. Sanitization to [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64} with hash fallback for empty. Counter at /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-<token>, lock at <counter>.lock (both regular files — flock is the SOLE acceptable lock primitive per OBJ-3). fd 9 opened INSIDE the command substitution via redirected brace-group per iteration-2 codex F1; parent shell branches on `[ "$decision" = "emit" ] || exit 0`. Saturated-counter branch (n>=3) refreshes counter mtime per F7 so >7d sweep cannot reset the rate-limit for long-lived sessions. Non-blocking guarantees applied per codex-review: bounded stdin `read -r -t 1 -d ''`, `timeout 2s` on every df call, `timeout 5s` wrapping the FULL du-awk-sort-head pipeline (not just du), and `flock -x -w 1` (bounded 1-second lock wait, skip on contention). PATH-resolved bare `df`/`du` per OBJ-5.

tasks_completed.description: Registered both new hooks in settings.json (6th SessionStart + new 4th UserPromptSubmit block) with hook-level timeouts
tasks_completed.rationale: AC7 — preserved existing 5 SessionStart hooks AND existing 3 UserPromptSubmit blocks in their original order; appended only. Added `"timeout": 5` to session-tmpfs-banner.sh and `"timeout": 15` to userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh per codex review F2 so the harness has a hard ceiling on either hook's runtime regardless of the script's internal time bounds.

tasks_completed.description: Rewrote /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh with EXCLUSION-FIRST, conditional dry-run output, IEC-i freed-bytes total, and per-category candidate logging
tasks_completed.rationale: AC4+AC5+AC6+AC7 — defined shared `EXCLUDE_PRUNE` array reused as `"${EXCLUDE_PRUNE[@]}"` at the start of every top-level /tmp find. The action (-print for dry-run / -print0 for real) lives INSIDE the right-hand non-prune branch via `-o \( "$@" -print \)` — never appended after the whole expression. Rewrote the run() helper as run_tmp() (top-level /tmp) and run_path() (claude-0 deep + /var/tmp carve-outs) per iteration-2 codex F3. Conditional `exec >> $LOG` on DRY_RUN=0; DRY_RUN=1 uses tee-to-BOTH-stdout-AND-log via `exec > >(tee -a "$LOG") 2>&1` (SOLE acceptable shape per OBJ-2). Removed chrome-debug-profile literal (original line 61) and happy-attachments literal (original line 86) from prune-target predicate lists; both paths still protected via shared EXCLUDE_PRUNE. Added new patterns at each age tier including *-scratch-[0-9]* (>3d so L3 convention is truthful) and claude-pressure-warn-* (>7d hook-state tier so the L1.5 counter+lock files don't leak). Per-mount freed=...K lines preserved unchanged; new IEC-i `freed_total=` line sums tmp+var deltas (negative-clamped to 0) via `numfmt --to=iec-i --suffix=B`. Codex-review fixes applied: F4 (candidate temp files now under /var/tmp not /tmp so a full /tmp doesn't prevent its own cleanup) and F5 (removed `| head -200` from dry-run paths so AC4/AC5 verification sees ALL candidates — confirmed 398 dev/test candidates visible after fix vs 200 before).

tasks_completed.description: Created L3 convention note for *-scratch-<timestamp> naming
tasks_completed.rationale: AC8 — 22-line note in docs/reference/ describing the *-scratch-<timestamp> convention. Explicitly states (a) the convention pattern, (b) that tmp-cleanup.sh sweeps `*-scratch-[0-9]*` at the >3d age tier (truthful — M3 added the pattern), (c) `no hook enforcement — convention only`, and (d) cross-reference to /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh.

scripts_created.purpose: Emit per-mount tmpfs Use% summary on SessionStart, with literal `WARN ` prefix at >75%
Emit non-blocking pressure warning on UserPromptSubmit when /tmp and/or /dev/shm > 75%, rate-limited ≤3/session via flock-protected counter file

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — enhancement, not a regression. The existing /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh was installed 2026-05-01 with intentionally narrow pattern coverage; this cycle extends it.
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Natural accumulation of Playwright profiles, codex outputs, hook state files, and dev/test scratch over time against a 4G/16G tmpfs budget. The original cleanup script's pattern list covered ~5 categories but missed ~12 codex-enumerated patterns; it also had no EXCLUSION-FIRST ordering, putting hard-exclusion path literals (`chrome-debug-profile` line 61, `happy-attachments` line 86) directly into prune-target predicate lists, and no visibility/early-warning surface for users.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: (L1) SessionStart banner makes baseline visible. (L1.5) Mid-session warning gives early feedback at >75% (well before ENOSPC). (L2) Broader pattern coverage + EXCLUSION-FIRST structural correctness + truthful freed-bytes total + dry-run stdout output for AC verification. (L3) Convention note makes future ad-hoc scratch directories self-cleaning.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Diff exceeds 20 lines because the spec requires 4 discrete deliverables (2 new hooks + 1 script rewrite + 1 doc) per the codex-debated 4-layer plan that the user explicitly approved with '实施全部'. Each deliverable is at its minimal viable shape: L1 hook is 39 lines (all non-comment lines are load-bearing for AC1/AC2); L1.5 hook is 147 lines (the flock+brace-group pattern from iteration-2 codex F1 is mandatory and verbose); L2 script grew from 100 to 261 lines because EXCLUSION-FIRST + 2 helper functions + 6 sweep categories + freed_total formula are spec-mandated; L3 doc is 22 lines covering 4 required claims. The total is bounded by the spec's explicit M1-M4 acceptance criteria; no over-engineering or refactoring beyond what the ticket prescribes.

dev.qa_notes: Verification approach: (1) AC1/AC2 — run new SessionStart hook and inspect stdout; use PATH-shim with fake `df` returning >75% Use% to verify WARN prefix per-line. (2) AC3 — invoke userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh directly with stdin JSON containing session_id, using a PATH-shim for `df --output=pcent` returning 85%/88% and a shim for `du -xh --max-depth=1`; verify 3 emissions then 4th silent for same session_id; also verify with DISTINCT session_ids each iteration emits (rate-limit is per-session); also verify the held-lock case (start a background flock holder, then invoke the hook — it should exit in ~1s with no output because flock -w 1 times out). (3) AC4/AC5 — run `/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh --dry-run`; grep stdout for the 7 category headers, and grep -E '(/tmp/chrome-debug-profile$|/tmp/happy-attachments$|/tmp/claude-0$|/tmp/claude-commit-plan-[a-zA-Z]+$|/tmp/happy-p05-cdp-[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$)' must return 0 matches (hard-exclusion paths absent from candidates); /tmp/claude-0/**/*.output candidates MAY appear (carve-out); verify the same output also appears in /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log under the latest dry_run=1 block. (4) AC6 — inspect /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log after a real run; look for the `freed_total=<IEC-i>` line and confirm per-mount `freed=...K` lines preserved. (5) AC7 — JSON-parse settings.json, confirm SessionStart[0].hooks has 6 entries in the original order ending with session-tmpfs-banner.sh, and UserPromptSubmit has 4 blocks in the original order ending with userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh; confirm /etc/cron.d/tmp-cleanup-daily is unchanged. (6) AC8 — grep -rn 'scratch' docs/reference/ and verify the convention note discoverable.

imported_by: all Claude Code sessions on this server — hooks fire on every session start and every user prompt
blast_radius.mitigation: Both new hooks exit 0 unconditionally; even pathological failure modes (df hang, du timeout, lock contention, stdin never-closing, numfmt missing) are bounded by `timeout 2s` / `timeout 5s` / `flock -w 1` / `read -t 1` / `|| true` fallbacks. Hook-level `"timeout": 5` and `"timeout": 15` in settings.json provide a hard outer bound enforced by the harness.

EXCLUSION-FIRST guarantee verified via dry-run grep against hard-exclusion paths (0 matches). Two-pass real-delete (find -print0 to tmpfile in /var/tmp, then log candidates from tmpfile, then xargs rm) avoids deletes on a corrupted candidate list. Per OBJ-2, dry-run output is observable on BOTH stdout and /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log — manual verification trivial.
New file; no callers other than the SessionStart registration.
New file; no callers other than the UserPromptSubmit registration.

imported_by: zero — documentation only, no code reference

implementation_notes: TodoWrite tool is denied for the dev role by pretool-tool-policy.v1 ("tool TodoWrite not in allowed_tools"). The harness emitted repeated reminders to use TodoWrite; per role-policy and the agent contract these were ignored.

Write tool was rejected by pretool-write-guard.sh when overwriting an existing file (tmp-cleanup.sh and tmp-cleanup-convention.md after creation). Worked around via Edit which is the documented harness convention for existing files.

Cleanup of test fixtures left behind in /tmp (banner-shim-*, l15-shim-*, test-scratch-20250101): `rm` is denied by pretool-bash-safety.sh for non-allowlisted paths. Fixtures are tiny (≤80 bytes each) and will be swept by the next daily cron via the new patterns (banner-shim and l15-shim do NOT match any cleanup pattern by name — they are time-bounded scratch and acceptable to leave for a future broader sweep; the test-scratch-20250101 fixture WILL be swept by the *-scratch-[0-9]* pattern at >3d, which is the intended end-to-end demonstration of the L3 convention).

self_verification.notes: Build/typecheck are not applicable (pure bash hooks + bash script). All ACs verified via stdout inspection, PATH-shim mocking for synthetic >75% scenarios (per OBJ-5 — no live tmpfs fill), JSON parsing for settings preservation, and direct grep against the modified script.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 actionable findings (F1-F5) + 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY (F6-F8). F1 (blocking flock — confirmed hook hangs under held-lock contention), F2 (unbounded df/du/stdin and missing hook-level timeouts), F4 (mktemp in /tmp during cleanup of /tmp — chicken-and-egg if /tmp is full), F5 (dry-run head -200 truncates AC4 candidate evidence): all classified `in_scope_real_bug` and applied. F3 (rate-limit reset for sessions where pressure drops then rises again >7d later): partial — the saturated-counter case (n>=3) is already handled by the spec-mandated F7 mtime refresh; the silent-during-pressure-drop case for 1<=n<=2 is out_of_scope (the cleanup design explicitly sweeps stale state at >7d; widening to refresh on every prompt regardless of pressure would change spec design). F3's lock-file-race-during-cleanup-delete sub-finding is out_of_scope (adjacent threat model). F6/F7/F8 OBSERVATION_ONLY logged without action. Codex output saved at /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-1014589-1779219737.txt.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied F1 (`flock -x -w 1 9`), F2 (`read -r -t 1 -d ''` for stdin, `timeout 2s` on all df calls, `timeout 5s` wrapping the full du-awk-sort-head pipeline via `bash -c`, settings.json `"timeout": 5` and `"timeout": 15` on the two new hooks), F4 (`mktemp -p /var/tmp` in both run_tmp and run_path with error fallback), F5 (removed `| head -200` from both helpers' dry-run paths). Verified post-fix: rate-limit still 3-then-silent; held-lock case exits in ~1.04s; dev/test tier dry-run now shows 398 candidates (vs 200 before F5). F3 partial scope decision documented here; F3 lock-race sub-finding documented as out_of_scope (the lock file is regular-file flock — Linux inode persists until last fd closes, so cleanup deletion mid-flock does not break the held lock; new invocations create a fresh lock file).

recommendations: Monitor /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log over the next 2-3 cron runs to confirm freed_total values are non-zero on real runs and that the broader-stale tier catches the codex-enumerated build/deploy patterns.

QA test driver (per OBJ-5) should use DISTINCT session_id for the 4 PATH-shim test iterations (e.g. `ba-qa-iter1`..`ba-qa-iter4`) and a separate FIFTH test reusing one session_id 4 times to verify the rate-limit itself.

Consider scheduling a one-off `/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh` (non-dry-run) within the next week to reap the existing accumulated patterns now that broader-stale tier is in place (398 dev/test candidates visible in dry-run — many of these are >7d old).
